title: Inventory Plan Fps Manifest Only Gates
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the 10 prior plan_fps manifest-only acceptance gates pinned at plan_fps/manifests/15-001-gate-plan-structure.json through plan_fps/manifests/15-010-gate-final-side-by-side.json and the 10 corresponding Bun:test acceptance gate tests at test/playable/acceptance/gate-plan-structure.test.ts through test/playable/acceptance/gate-final-side-by-side.test.ts. Each of the 10 gates pins a single new oracle identifier in the plan_fps namespace OR-FPS-037..OR-FPS-046 that is registered in plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md alongside the inherited OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-036 oracles surfaced by the prior 01-015 fixture inventory and the prior 01-016 fixture-locking parity-test inventory. Every gate manifest declares a schemaVersion=1 envelope, a commandContract pinning the runtime command bun run doom.ts (program=bun, subcommand=run, entryFile=doom.ts), an evidenceHash that locks the canonical manifest content, a transition block linking it to its prior gate (15-001 has no prior gate beyond the prerequisite 14-007 smoke-test-clean-local-working-tree; 15-010 has no next gate because it is the final gate), a deterministicReplayCompatibility block confirming the gate evaluation does not advance simulation tics, mutate the input stream, mutate the random seed, or require live wall-clock timing, and an oracleId for the manifest itself plus the underlying captured-oracle scope each gate accepts (OR-FPS-009 for 15-003 title-frame, OR-FPS-010/015..023 for 15-004 menu-navigation, OR-FPS-024 for 15-005 e1m1-start, OR-FPS-025..030 for 15-006 input-replay, OR-FPS-020/032/033 for 15-007 audio, OR-FPS-022/031 for 15-008 save-load, OR-FPS-011/012/013/014/034/035 for 15-009 attract-loop-and-long-run, and OR-FPS-036/032/033/034/035 for 15-010 final-side-by-side). Each gate test under test/playable/acceptance/ asserts the manifest schema verbatim, recomputes the evidenceHash with sha256 over the manifest sub-document, cross-checks the plan_fps/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md transition lines, and confirms the registration row exists in plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md. The inventory captures filename, relative path, on-disk size, line count, SHA-256 fingerprint, and the four cross-references each gate carries (manifestPath, acceptanceTestPath, manifestOracleId, manifestEvidenceHash) without embedding proprietary id Software bytes from any source under doom/, iwad/, or reference/. The oracle namespace OR-FPS-037..OR-FPS-046 is allocated by the prior plan_fps acceptance gates and is non-overlapping with the OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-036 fixture oracles inventoried in 01-015 and 01-016, so plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 acceptance gates 13-001..13-003 must allocate new identifiers above OR-FPS-046 rather than collide with these registered acceptance-gate oracle identifiers.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using Bun.Glob-equivalent plan_fps/manifests/15-*.json and test/playable/acceptance/gate-*.test.ts file enumeration, statSync for on-disk size_bytes, line counting via readFileSync split('\n') with trailing-newline normalization, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints over the raw file bytes, JSON parsing of every plan_fps/manifests/15-*.json gate manifest to extract the canonical commandContract block (runtimeCommand, program, subcommand, entryFile), the canonical evidenceHash sha256 hex string, the canonical schemaVersion integer, the canonical deterministicReplayCompatibility block (with the closed-enum {advancesSimulationTics|simulationTickAdvanced|simulationTickMutation|simulationTicMutation|simulationTickMutated, mutatesInputStream|inputStreamMutation|inputStreamMutated, mutatesRandomSeed|randomSeedMutation|randomSeedMutated, requiresLiveClock|requiresWallClockTiming|requiresLiveAudio|saveFileBytesRedistributed} field-name vocabulary observed across the 10 manifests), the gate identifier (15-001..15-010) and gate title (gate-plan-structure..gate-final-side-by-side), the new oracle identifier each manifest registers (OR-FPS-037..OR-FPS-046), the prior gate transition (fromStepId or previousStepIdentifier), the next gate transition (toStepId or nextStepIdentifier), the captured-oracle scope each gate accepts (OR-FPS-* identifiers from the underlying fixture-locking tests inventoried by 01-015 and 01-016), Grep-equivalent regex extraction of the OR-FPS-### registration row in plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md, plan_fps/MASTER_CHECKLIST.md transition-line existence checks for every gate, plan_vanilla_parity/steps/13-* file existence checks for the Phase 13 final-proof follow-up step IDs, and package.json Bun script context.

command_contract_invariants.notes: Every plan_fps/manifests/15-*.json gate manifest declares the same Bun command contract — every gate is bound to the bun run doom.ts runtime command, the bun program, the run subcommand, and the doom.ts entry file. No plan_fps manifest-only gate accepts an alternative runtime path (no node, ts-node, npx, or compiled binary). The plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 final-proof acceptance gates 13-001..13-003 must preserve this exact command contract because the Bun-only runtime constraint is also pinned by AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md and is non-negotiable across all gates.

deterministic_replay_compatibility_invar.notes: Every plan_fps/manifests/15-*.json gate manifest pins a deterministicReplayCompatibility block declaring the four invariants above (no simulation-tic advance, no input-stream mutation, no random-seed mutation, no live-clock or audio or save-file-redistribution requirement during gate evaluation). The exact field names vary across the 10 manifests because they were authored as separate Ralph-loop steps — the closed-enum vocabulary captured here records every field name observed across the 10 manifests so the focused inventory test can detect any drift if a future plan_fps step adds a new manifest with a synonym. The plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 final-proof acceptance gates 13-001..13-003 should standardize on a single canonical block shape rather than continue allowing synonyms because tooling that hashes the gate evidence depends on exact field-name agreement.

oracle_namespace_origin.notes: The 10 plan_fps manifest-only gates each register a single new oracle identifier in the plan_fps namespace OR-FPS-037 through OR-FPS-046, allocated immediately above the OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-036 fixture-locking oracle range surfaced by the prior 01-015 inventory and pinned by the 01-016 fixture-locking parity-test inventory. Together OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-046 cover every oracle the prior plan_fps work pinned, with the manifest-only acceptance gates occupying the contiguous 037..046 sub-range. plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md is the authoritative registry for these identifiers and remains read-only under plan_vanilla_parity governance. plan_vanilla_parity/PARALLEL_WORK.md owns the inventory lane and the future Phase 13 acceptance lane that will allocate new identifiers above OR-FPS-046 rather than reuse any inherited identifier in the 006..046 range. The gate manifests deliberately do NOT register OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-008 nor any underlying captured-oracle identifier as their own manifest_oracle_id — they accept those captured oracles via captured_oracle_scope_sorted and pin a separate manifest_oracle_id for the manifest itself.

boundary_status.notes: plan_fps/manifests/ is a writable manifest workspace inherited from the prior plan_fps Ralph-loop, but plan_vanilla_parity governance treats plan_fps/ as prior art only — no Ralph-loop step under plan_vanilla_parity may modify the 10 plan_fps/manifests/15-*.json gate manifests inventoried here. test/playable/acceptance/ is the writable test workspace where the 10 acceptance gate tests live; this inventory captures only filename, relative path, size, line count, SHA-256 fingerprint of the test files, plus the manifest cross-references each test enforces. No proprietary id Software bytes are embedded; the manifests record only the bun run doom.ts command contract, evidenceHash sha256 strings, oracle identifiers, gate transitions, and references to test/oracles/fixtures/capture-*.json fixtures whose content metadata is owned by the prior 01-015 inventory.

implications: The 10 plan_fps manifest-only gates form an inherited acceptance contract that the plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 final-proof gates 13-001 gate-shareware-doom-one-full-playthrough, 13-002 gate-registered-doom-user-supplied-iwad-scope, and 13-003 gate-ultimate-doom-user-supplied-iwad-scope must reconcile with: each Phase 13 gate must either (a) inherit one of the 15-001..15-010 manifests verbatim, (b) replace it with a Phase 13 manifest that pins a new oracle identifier above OR-FPS-046, or (c) declare the inherited manifest superseded with explicit traceability in plan_vanilla_parity/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md.

Every plan_fps manifest-only gate pins the runtime command bun run doom.ts via a commandContract block declaring program=bun, subcommand=run, and entryFile=doom.ts. plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 acceptance gates must preserve this exact command contract because the Bun-only runtime constraint is also pinned by AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md and the doom.ts root entrypoint is pinned by plan_vanilla_parity/steps/03-001-add-root-doom-ts-bun-entrypoint.md.

The deterministicReplayCompatibility block in every gate manifest declares the same four invariants (no simulation-tic advance during gate evaluation, no input-stream mutation, no random-seed mutation, no live-clock or audio or save-file-redistribution requirement) but uses 23 different field names across the 10 manifests because they were authored independently. plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 acceptance gates should standardize on a single canonical block shape rather than continue allowing synonyms; the focused inventory test for this step locks the observed vocabulary so a future canonicalization step can detect drift.

The 10 manifest_oracle_ids OR-FPS-037..OR-FPS-046 are non-overlapping with the OR-FPS-006..OR-FPS-036 captured-oracle range owned by 01-015 and 01-016. Future plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-capture steps that allocate new oracle identifiers must continue above OR-FPS-046 to avoid silent collision with the registered acceptance-gate manifest identifiers. plan_fps/REFERENCE_ORACLES.md remains the authoritative registry for the inherited 006..046 sub-range and is read-only under plan_vanilla_parity governance.

Each gate manifest's transition block records its prior gate (15-001 has the prerequisite 14-007 smoke-test-clean-local-working-tree; 15-002..15-010 each cite the immediately prior 15-NNN gate) and its next gate (15-001..15-009 each cite the immediately next 15-NNN gate; 15-010 records null because it is the final gate). plan_vanilla_parity Phase 13 acceptance gates inherit this linear ordering and must not introduce a parallel branch through the 15-* gates that would diverge from the inherited transition chain.

Two gate manifests (15-001 gate-plan-structure and 15-002 gate-bun-launch-smoke) declare empty captured_oracle_scope_sorted lists because their gate evaluation rests on plan-structure and runtime-launch evidence rather than captured-fixture evidence. The other eight gates (15-003..15-010) reference one or more OR-FPS-009..OR-FPS-036 captured oracles inventoried by 01-015 and locked by 01-016, so any plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-capture step that replaces a captured fixture's pending status must coordinate with both the 01-015 inventory snapshot and this 01-017 manifest-only-gate inventory.

Because each manifest and each acceptance test records its on-disk SHA-256 fingerprint in this inventory, any structural rewrite of a gate manifest (such as a global formatter sweep that changes JSON whitespace, an evidenceHash recomputation triggered by a manifest schema change, or a Prettier-style key reordering) will diverge from the captured snapshot and require a follow-up update to this inventory. The focused test enforces that divergence by recomputing each fingerprint at test time; a stale inventory will fail rather than silently mask a manifest rewrite that breaks the gate.
